behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watch/path emission), not just local logic. MOCK-MISS: unit parsing tests cannot prove subdirectory events are emitted by OS watcher. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can hide platform-specific recursive-watch gaps. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp watched root, create a nested subdirectory and write a file inside it, then assert exactly one transformed output appears in output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is module handoff/state propagation: request intake + dedup store + configurable window. MOCK-MISS: pure unit of a helper function may miss config wiring/time-source interaction. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess for this behavior text; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because behavior is phrased as processing-request dedup logic, not OS event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process intake and dedup modules with a test clock/config, submit two requests for the same normalized file path within the window, and assert only one pipeline execution is triggered; then advance beyond window and assert a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references OS emitting duplicate events; real failure appears at OS watcher boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher often emits idealized single events and misses duplicate/coalesced patterns. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model-dependent correctness or agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple FS events (e.g., temp-write + rename or rapid append/close), capture pipeline invocations, and assert exactly one processing of that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS/filesystem lifecycle behavior (delete/recreate inode/path watch invalidation). In-process fakes rarely reproduce watcher detachment/rebind edge cases. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon on a real temp directory, remove the watched directory while running, recreate it at same path, then write a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file timestamp metadata and concurrent arrival timing; this can diverge from mocked/in-memory ordering. Component coherence: this is cross-module handoff (watcher->metadata->scheduler), and the boundary includes OS timestamp semantics, so Integration is insufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files nearly simultaneously in the watched directory using real filesystem operations, record observed processing order, and assert it matches creation-time ordering (including deterministic tie-break rule if timestamps collide).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real write-in-progress behavior (partial writes, close/flush timing, possible locks) plus watcher timing. MOCK-HIDE: fake FS usually lacks true in-progress write semantics and race timing. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependency or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, open a file in watched directory, write partial content and keep descriptor open, trigger/observe arrival event, verify no processing starts until writer closes file, then assert processing runs once and reads full final content.
